title: Bridge Essay: Crossing the invisible line: De-differentiation of wake, sleep and dreaming may

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Crossing, the, invisible, line:, De-differentiation.

**Crossing the invisible line: De-differentiation of wake, sleep and dreaming may engender both creative insight and psychopathology.** — Conscious Cogn. PMID: 27718406. DOI: 10.1016/j.concog.2016.09.018. Writing about dreaming, the poet Raymond Carver said "Ifeel as if I've crossed some kind of invisible line". In creative people, the "line" between wake, dreaming and psychopathology may be porous, engendering ade-differentiated, super-critical, hybrid state. Evidence exists for arelationship between creativity and psychopathology but its nature has been elusive. De-differentiation between wake, sleep and dreaming may be the common substrate, as dream-like cognition pervades wake and wake-lik
